question_title: Will Onn Hafiz Ghazi win the Machap seat in the 2026 Johor state election?
summary: Onn Hafiz Ghazi, the caretaker Johor Menteri Besar and BN/UMNO chairman, defends his Machap stronghold (won 2022 with 56.6%, majority 6,543). The 2026 field structure is strongly favorable: Machap is a straight BN vs PH/AMANAH fight with PN NOT contesting, and PAS has instructed supporters to back BN where PN isn't standing — consolidating the Malay-Muslim conservative vote (~65% Malay electorate) behind Onn and neutralizing the main 'green wave' loss scenario. BN is heavily favored statewide (analyst projection 36-42 of 56 seats). The residual risk is a consolidated PH challenger drawing the ~28% Chinese vote plus a youth/protest swing (Machap was competitive in the 2018 anti-BN wave with a 404 majority), plus low-turnout variance. But a popular sitting MB losing his own stronghold would be a major upset. Holding at 0.93, slightly below the factor-implied ~0.97 to respect irreducible single-seat election uncertainty.

factors.description: Onn Hafiz won Machap 2022 with 56.6% and majority of 6,543 in a 4-way race; strong baseline (per question description)
Incumbent Menteri Besar and Johor BN chairman, widely described as popular; sitting MBs almost never lose their own stronghold seat
2026 Machap is a straight BN-PH fight with PN NOT contesting, and PAS instructing supporters to back BN — consolidates Malay-Muslim conservative vote behind Onn, neutralizing green-wave risk
BN heavily favored statewide (analyst BN 36-42 of 56); favorable political environment in Johor
Machap historically competitive (404 majority in 2018 anti-BN wave); consolidated PH challenger could draw Chinese + youth + disaffected Malay vote, plus low-turnout variance

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.93 (YES)**

Onn Hafiz Ghazi is very likely to retain the Machap seat. The key drivers:

- **Strong baseline**: He won Machap in 2022 with 56.6% (majority 6,543) in a four-way contest.
- **Favorable field structure**: The 2026 race is a straight BN-vs-PH/AMANAH fight. Critically, PN is *not* contesting Machap (one of 23 seats it sits out), and PAS has told supporters to back BN where PN isn't standing — this consolidates the ~65% Malay conservative vote behind Onn and removes the main "green wave" loss scenario.
- **Incumbency + profile**: As caretaker Menteri Besar and Johor BN chairman, he's the face of the campaign and widely described as popular. Sitting MBs rarely lose their own stronghold.
- **Statewide tailwind**: BN is heavily favored (analysts project 36-42 of 56 seats).

The residual ~7% accounts for genuine downside: Machap was competitive in the 2018 anti-BN wave (404-vote majority), a consolidated PH could capture the ~28% Chinese vote plus a youth/protest swing, and low projected turnout (45-50%) adds variance. But a popular incumbent MB losing his own seat would be a major upset — hence the high probability.
resolution_criteria: This question resolves YES if Onn Hafiz Ghazi is declared the winning candidate for the Machap (N26) state constituency in the 11 July 2026 Johor state election. It resolves NO if any other candidate is declared the winner of the Machap seat. Resolution will be based on the official result for Machap as reported on the Wikipedia page "Machap (state constituency)" (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Machap_(state_constituency)) or the "2026 Johor state election" page (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Johor_state_election). If those pages do not yet show the result, resolution will follow the official Election Commission of Malaysia (SPR) declaration as reported by credible sources (https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions).
fine_print: "Winning candidate" means the candidate the returning officer officially declares elected for Machap (N26), including any result determined after a recount. If Onn Hafiz Ghazi does not contest the Machap seat (e.g., he is replaced as candidate or contests elsewhere) such that he is not on the Machap ballot, the question resolves NO. If polling for Machap is postponed beyond 11 July 2026 (for example due to the death of a candidate triggering a fresh nomination process) so that no official winner is declared before 12 July 2026, the question resolves NO.

factors.description: Onn Hafiz won Machap 2022 with 56.6% and majority of 6,543 in a 4-way race; strong baseline
Incumbent Menteri Besar and Johor BN chairman, widely described as popular; sitting MBs almost never lose their own stronghold seat
2026 Machap is a straight BN-PH fight with PN NOT contesting, and PAS instructing supporters to back BN — consolidates Malay-Muslim conservative vote behind Onn, neutralizing green-wave risk
BN heavily favored statewide (analyst BN 36-42 of 56); favorable political environment in Johor
Machap historically competitive (404 majority in 2018 anti-BN wave); consolidated PH challenger could draw Chinese + youth + disaffected Malay vote

input.anchor: Incumbent MB defending a stronghold won by 56.6% / majority 6,543: base rate of such incumbents holding is very high (~88-92%)
input.assessment: Onn Hafiz is the caretaker MB defending his stronghold seat. Field structure is strongly favorable: PN sits out Machap and PAS backs BN, so the main loss scenario (Malay vote splitting to PN) is gone. PH/AMANAH is the sole challenger in a straight fight, but PH consolidating all opposition (Chinese ~28% + youth + some Malay) still falls well short of his 56.6% base when PN's former conservative voters lean BN. A sitting MB losing his own seat would be a major upset. Main residual risk is a youth/protest swing plus low-turnout surprises, kept as a moderate downward factor.
input.calibration_notes: Anchor ~88%. Favorable field structure (PN out, PAS backs BN) pushes up; residual competitiveness/youth risk pulls slightly down. Net ~93%. Not hedging — evidence genuinely strongly favors YES.
input.key_uncertainties: Whether a consolidated PH plus youth protest swing could overcome a popular incumbent MB in a straight fight; turnout composition.
input.tool_audit: research() sub-agent returned rich, well-sourced findings (Malay Mail, Star, Oriental Daily, FMT) confirming the straight-fight structure and PAS-backs-BN dynamic. No tool failures. No formal scientific poll exists, so seat figures are analyst projections.
input.process_reflection: The research tool was efficient and returned exactly the campaign-structure detail needed (who contests Machap, PAS positioning). For a single-seat incumbent question this was sufficient; no Monte Carlo needed given the categorical favorability. Scaffolding worked smoothly.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
I verified the factors against the research trace, the reflection.yaml, the worldview research entries, and independent web search. The forecast is fundamentally sound; I found one detail-level grounding gap worth flagging.

WHAT'S WELL-GROUNDED:
- Core field-structure claims (Factors 2, 3, 4) are directly supported by the research trace: Onn Hafiz is caretaker MB / Johor BN-UMNO chairman defending Machap (his seat since March 2022, confirmed independently); Machap is a straight BN vs PH/AMANAH fight with PN NOT contesting; PAS instructed supporters to back BN where PN isn't standing; analysts project BN 36-42 of 56. All cited to Malay Mail, Star, Oriental Daily, FMT, Zaobao.
- Resolution criteria are provided and well-engaged: the agent addresses the named Wikipedia source, the on-ballot requirement (nomination concluded 27 June, all 56 seats contested, no walkovers), and the postponement edge case is moot.
- Counterargument is strong, not a token gesture — it builds a coherent alternative story (2018's 404-vote majority showing genuine competitiveness + consolidated anti-BN vote + youth kingmakers + low-turnout variance).
- Tool audit "no tool failures" matches ground truth (0 errors / 16 calls). The one failed 'research' call in the trace was an immediately-corrected tool-name typo, not counted in metrics.

THE WARN (unsupported detail):
- Factor 1 and the anchor both cite "Onn Hafiz won Machap 2022 with 56.6% and majority of 6,543." But the research result in the trace EXPLICITLY states: "a precise 2022 Machap majority figure wasn't available in the sources." Within the trace snapshot I was given (which ends at "Let me run the premortem"), this exact figure has no support. The tool metrics show 7 wikipedia calls and 5 fetch_url calls that occur AFTER this snapshot and are not visible to me — these almost certainly recovered the figure from the Machap constituency Wikipedia page (the named resolution source), and the number is a real, verifiable historical fact. The CONCLUSION that moves the forecast — that Machap is a decisive BN stronghold win for Onn — is well-grounded regardless. So this is an unsupported-detail issue (warn), not a forecast-changing hallucination.

PROBABILITY ASSESSMENT:
The 93% is well-calibrated. The anchor (88-92% for an incumbent MB defending a stronghold) is well-sourced, and the agent's modest push above midpoint is justified by a genuinely favorable field structure (PN out + PAS backing BN removes the main loss scenario of Malay vote-splitting). The −0.6 downside factor appropriately captures the residual youth/turnout/competitiveness risk. My independent estimate lands ~90-93%; no meaningful divergence. Mild overlap between Factors 3 and 4 (both reflect favorable BN environment) is not egregious since one is Machap-specific and one statewide.

No double-counting that distorts the estimate, no wrong-direction factors, no contradictory assessment. Recommend the agent attach the specific 56.6%/6,543 source (the wikipedia call result) to Factor 1 so the figure is traceable.
